title: P1(2) — wire the supersede (versioning) action across all entities with a superseded state
business_reason: 22 entities define a 'superseded' lifecycle state (templates, keys, snapshots, invoices, batches, results, certificates) but no supersede action was generated, so versioning/replacement was unreachable.
brain_skills: Skills: 04 Lifecycle, 08 Security. Master loop: extend the generator maps, regenerate, guards enforce from-states.

param_14.question: Generate the supersede action wherever a superseded state exists.
param_14.answer: Added superseded->supersede (approve) to both STATUS_ACTION maps; added supersede to gen_ui REASON_VERBS + DANGER_VERBS (destructive replacement -> reason + danger-confirm). 13 primary services now expose supersede; gen_guards constrains from-states from workflows.json.
param_14.security_implications: supersede is approve-class (reason required) + danger-confirm; from-states guarded.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: supersede EFFECTS (cloning to a new version row, re-pointing children) are per-domain and tracked for the relevant P1 module CRs; this wires the action + guard + audit.

principles_pass.P0.6: one map addition wires versioning everywhere

functional: supersede action generated on superseded-capable services; guards constrain from-states
regression: full e2e 29 passed/0 failed; unit 166/166; tsc clean

verification: tsc clean; unit 166/166; full e2e green (29 passed, 1 skipped); drift clean.

known_followups.supersede_effects: per-domain version-clone + child re-point in P1 module CRs
